questions.question: The power to enlarge the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court of India with respect to any matter included in the Union List of Legislative Powers rests with:
questions.option_4: The Union Ministry of Law, Justice and Company Affairs
questions.solution: Under Article 138 of the Constitution, the Parliament has the power to enlarge the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court with respect to any of the matters in the Union List. Furthermore, the Parliament can extend the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court in other matters through a special agreement between the Government of India and the Government of any State.

questions.question: The term of the Lok Sabha
questions.option_1: cannot be extended under any circumstances
questions.option_2: can be extended by six months at a time
questions.option_3: can be extended by one year at a time during the proclamation of emergency
questions.option_4: can be extended for two years at a time during proclamation of emergency
questions.solution:
The normal term of the Lok Sabha is five years, but it can be modified under specific conditions:

  • Extension: While a Proclamation of National Emergency (Article 352) is in operation, the life of the Lok Sabha may be extended by a law of Parliament for a period not exceeding one year at a time.
  • Limit: There is no limit on the number of times it can be extended, but the extension cannot continue beyond a period of six months after the emergency has ceased to operate.

questions.question: In what way does the Indian Parliament exercise control over the administration?
questions.option_2: Through Consultative Committees of various ministries
questions.option_3: By making the administrators send periodic reports
questions.option_4: By compelling the executive to issue writs
questions.solution:
The Indian Parliament exercises control over the administration (the Executive) through various mechanisms to ensure accountability:

  • Parliamentary Committees: This is the most effective way Parliament exercises day-to-day control. Committees like the Public Accounts Committee, Estimates Committee, and Departmentally Related Standing Committees (DRSCs) scrutinize the work, expenditures, and policies of various ministries in detail.
  • Other Methods: These include Question Hour, motions (adjournment, censure, no-confidence), and discussions. Note that 'Consultative Committees' are not parliamentary committees; they are attached to ministries and chaired by the respective Ministers.

questions.question: The Speaker can ask a member of the House to stop speaking and let another member speak. This phenomenon is known as
questions.solution:
In parliamentary procedure, specific terms describe the conduct of members:

  • Yielding the Floor: This occurs when the Speaker requests a member to stop speaking to allow another member to speak.
  • Crossing the Floor: Passing between the member addressing the House and the Chair, or changing one's party allegiance.
  • Interpellation: The formal right of a Parliament to submit formal questions to the government.
  • Decorum: The requirement that members maintain proper behavior and observe the rules of the House.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements about a Money Bill is not correct?
questions.option_1: A Money Bill can be tabled in either House of Parliament
questions.option_2: The Speaker of Lok Sabha is the final authority to decide whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not
questions.option_3: The Rajya Sabha must return a Money Bill passed by the Lok Sabha and send it for consideration within 14 days
questions.option_4: The President cannot return a Money Bill to the Lok Sabha for reconsideration
questions.solution:
The constitutional provisions for a Money Bill (Article 110) include the following strict rules:

  • Introduction: A Money Bill cannot be tabled in either House; it can only be introduced in the Lok Sabha on the recommendation of the President.
  • Certification: If any question arises whether a bill is a Money Bill or not, the decision of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha is final.
  • Rajya Sabha's Role: The Rajya Sabha must return the bill within 14 days with or without recommendations.
  • Presidential Assent: The President can either give assent or withhold assent to a Money Bill but cannot return it for reconsideration, as it is introduced with his/her prior permission.
